attributes.action: Develop a managed off‑road vehicle site at Sundon Pits, Bedfordshire
attributes.additional_details: A proposal will be submitted to our local MP asking to assess Sundon Pits for controlled off‑road use. This sites current unmanaged use presents hazardous antisocial behaviour, environmental damage, noise pollution, fly‑tipping, with repeated police, and fire call‑outs. Evidence from regulated sites elsewhere in England such as Parkwood Outdoors or Hawkstone Park show managed off‑road provision reduces illegal riding, harm, and emergency service use; schemes supported by Natural England/RSPB.
attributes.background: A regulated facility offers a long‑term solution balancing recreation with conservation. Although complex and initially costly, a managed approach reduces ongoing public spending and protects sensitive habitats. Providing off-road enthusiasts a safe place to enjoy their activities locally.

rejection.details:
We can’t accept petitions that call for action at a local level.

As you mentioned, you could ask your MP to take action on your concerns about this issue, including by asking them to present a public (paper) petition about this.

You can find contact details for your local MP here: https://members.parliament.uk/FindYourMP

And you can find out how to ask your MP to present a public (paper) petition here: https://www.parliament.uk/get-involved/sign-a-petition/paper-petitions/

You could also raise your issue with a local councillor who represents you. You can use this page to find out who your local councillors are, and how to contact them: https://www.gov.uk/find-your-local-councillors
